elements.sent-2411-001: We investigate the degrees of freedom of New General Relativity.
elements.sent-2411-002: This theory is a three-parameter theory and is classified into nine irreducible types according to the rotation symmetry of $SO(3)$ on each leaf of ADM-foliation.
elements.sent-2411-003: In the previous work~[{\it 2410.15056[gr-qc] ], we investigated the degrees of freedom in types of NGR that are interests for describing gravity: Type 2, Type 3, Type 5, and Type 8.}
elements.sent-2411-004: In this work, we focus on unveiling those numbers in all other types to complete the analysis of NGR.
elements.sent-2411-005: After providing the Hamiltonian formulation of NGR, we perform the analysis on Type 4, Type 7, and Type 9 according to the method that is provided in the previous work~[{\it 2410.15056[gr-qc] ].}
elements.sent-2411-006: Then we unveil that the degrees of freedom of Type 4, Type 7, and Type 9 are five, null, and three, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-007: Type 4 and Type 9 have second-class constraint densities only.
elements.sent-2411-008: Type 7 has first-class constraint densities only, but which is over constraint.
elements.sent-2411-009: In every type, no bifurcation occurs unlikely to Type 8 in the previous work~[{\it 2410.15056[gr-qc] ].}
elements.sent-2411-010: Finally, we summarize this work and give a concluding remark for this series of works.
elements.sent-2411-011: Introduction Metric-Affine gauge theories of Gravity (MAG) are a candidate for going beyond General Relativity (GR) to reconcile the recent observational issues in cosmology such as inflation~\cite{Planck:2018vyg,Vazquez:2018qdg,Tsujikawa:2003jp , dark matter~\cite{Freese:2008cz,Planck:2018vyg,Billard:2021uyg , dark energy (the late-time accelerated expansion of the universe)~\cite{SupernovaSearchTeam:1998fmf,SupernovaCosmologyProject:1998vns,Planck:2018vyg , the tension problem in cosmological parameters~\cite{Planck:2018vyg,H0LiCOW:2019pvv,Riess:2019cxk,Schoneberg:2022ggi,ACT:2023kun .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-012: MAG is composed of independent classes according to the imposition of gauge conditions~\cite{Hehl:1994ue,Blagojevic:2002du,Adak:2005cd,Adak:2006rx,Adak:2008gd,Adak:2011ltj,Bahamonde:2021gfp,Tomonari:2023ars .}
elements.sent-2411-013: In a set of gauge conditions for vanishing non-metricity, MAG leads to GR and TEGR, in which gravity is described by using the curvature of the Levi-Civita connection and the torsion under the imposition of vanishing generic curvature (teleparallel condition), respectively~\cite{Bahamonde:2021gfp , and also to a hybrid class of these geometric quantities is known as Poincare's gauge theory of gravity~\cite{Hehl:1976kj,Hayashi:1981fx,Blagojevic:2003cg,Hehl:2023khc .}}
elements.sent-2411-014: TEGR can be extended using free parameters to the so-called New General Relativity (NGR)~\cite{Hayashi:1979qx .}
elements.sent-2411-015: The Degrees of Freedom (DoF) counting of NGR is the purpose of this series of papers.
elements.sent-2411-016: This is an important subject in the applications of perturbation theories to cosmology and astrophysics, since only when the strong couplings and ghost modes are absent, the theory predicts and explains physical phenomena in a healthy manner~\cite{Gomes:2023tur,Bahamonde:2024zkb .}
elements.sent-2411-017: Unveiling the DoF of a given theory relates deeply to the investigation to the former issue.
elements.sent-2411-018: In the previous work~\cite{Tomonari:2024ybs , we counted the nonlinear DoF of NGR focusing on Type 2, Type 3, Type 5, and Type 8 applying the Dirac-Bergmann analysis~\cite{Dirac:1950pj,Dirac:1958sq,Bergmann:1949zz,BergmannBrunings1949,Bergmann1950,Anderson:1951ta .}}
elements.sent-2411-019: These types have a motivation for describing gravity by virtue of the inclusion of gravitational propagating modes~\cite{Bahamonde:2024zkb .}
elements.sent-2411-020: Then, we concluded that all types suffer from the existence of ghost mode or strong coupling at least in the {\it linear perturbation theory around the Minkowski background spacetime.}
elements.sent-2411-021: However, note that this pathology does not indicate the fault of the theory of gravity itself but that of the perturbation theory.
elements.sent-2411-022: Thus, the results suggest only that we should investigate other background spacetime for the perturbations and/or alternate the linear perturbation theory by other candidates.
elements.sent-2411-023: In addition, in the previous work~\cite{Tomonari:2024ybs , we also unveiled that a set of specific conditions on Lagrange multipliers may cause bifurcation(s) in the theory, and we define a new class of constraint densities, labeled as a semi-first class constraint density.}
elements.sent-2411-024: In the current paper, we focus on the remaining types, {\it i.e., Type 4, Type 7, and Type 9.}
elements.sent-2411-025: It is a well-known fact that these types do not contain gravitational propagating modes~\cite{Bahamonde:2024zkb .}
elements.sent-2411-026: In the current paper, we will show that in these types all constraint densities are classified either as second-class (Type 4 and Type 9) or first-class with over-constraint (Type 7), and such bifurcation does not occur in all types.
elements.sent-2411-027: Namely, there is no room for any semi-first class constraint density to appear that is unlikely to be Type 8~\cite{Tomonari:2024ybs .}
elements.sent-2411-028: Therefore, these types are ruled out for describing gravity, but as will be discussed in conclusion of the current paper, these types would still provide dynamical models in other research fields.
elements.sent-2411-029: In particular, in Type 7, it is possible to use for a dynamical system equipped with a local $SO(3)$-symmetry in which single nonlinear DoF exists, fixing properly the gauge.
elements.sent-2411-030: The construction of the current paper is given as follows.
elements.sent-2411-031: In Sec.~\ref{02 , we summarize the fundamental ingredients of NGR in the $SO(3)$-irreducible decomposition of canonical momentum to perform the analysis.}
elements.sent-2411-032: In Sec.~\ref{03 , the Dirac-Bergmann analysis is performed on each type of theory.}

elements.sent-2411-034: We unveil that the DoF of Type 4, Type 7, and Type 9 are five, null DoF, and three, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-035: There is no bifurcation due to the property of Lagrange multipliers, differing from the case of Type 8 in the previous work~\cite{Tomonari:2024ybs .}
elements.sent-2411-036: Finally, in Sec.~\ref{04 , we conclude this series of works.}

elements.sent-2411-102: If the local Lorentz symmetry, on one hand, holds then for any observer a {\it global foliation always exists and it does not depend on an observer.}
elements.sent-2411-103: On the other hand, if the local Lorentz symmetry is broken then a {\it global foliation generically depends on each observer.}
elements.sent-2411-104: It might imply that the result of the Dirac-Bergmann analysis of each type of NGR gives rise to further bifurcation.

elements.sent-2411-114: We investigate degrees of freedom in New General Relativity.
elements.sent-2411-115: This theory is the three-parameter extension of Teleparallel Equivalent to GR and classified into nine irreducible types according to the rotation symmetry $SO(3)$ on each leaf of ADM-foliation.
elements.sent-2411-116: In the previous work~[{\it 2410.15056[gr-qc] ], we investigated the degrees of freedom in NGR types that are of interest in describing gravity: Type 2, Type 3, Type 5, and Type 8.}
elements.sent-2411-117: After providing the Hamiltonian formulation of NGR and considering in detail the regularity of NGR, we perform the analysis of Type 4, Type 7, and Type 9.
elements.sent-2411-118: We reveal that the degrees of freedom of Type 4, Type 7, and Type 9 are five, zero (purely topological system in bulk spacetime), and three, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-119: Type 4 and Type 9 have second-class constraint densities only, whereas Type 7 has first-class constraint densities only.
elements.sent-2411-120: In every type, no bifurcation occurs.
elements.sent-2411-121: In particular, Type 4 and Type 7 are irregular and provide specific examples of handling irregular systems.
elements.sent-2411-122: Since no general method is known for treating an irregular system, this work contributes to furthering the understanding of irregular systems.
elements.sent-2411-123: MAG is composed of independent classes according to gauge conditions~\cite{Hehl:1994ue,Blagojevic:2002du,Adak:2005cd,Adak:2006rx,Adak:2008gd,Adak:2011ltj,Bahamonde:2021gfp,Tomonari:2023ars .}
elements.sent-2411-124: In a set of gauge conditions for vanishing non-metricity, MAG leads to GR and TEGR, in which gravity is described by using the curvature of the Levi-Civita connection and the torsion under the imposition of vanishing generic curvature (teleparallel condition), respectively~\cite{Bahamonde:2021gfp .}
elements.sent-2411-125: A hybrid theory of these geometric quantities is known as Poincare's gauge theory of gravity~\cite{Hehl:1976kj,Hayashi:1981fx,Blagojevic:2003cg,Hehl:2023khc .}
elements.sent-2411-126: TEGR can be extended using three-free parameters into New General Relativity (NGR)~\cite{Hayashi:1979qx .}
elements.sent-2411-127: Counting Degrees of Freedom (DoF) of NGR is the purpose of this series of papers.
elements.sent-2411-128: Unveiling the DoFs of a given theory relate deeply to the investigation of these issues.
elements.sent-2411-129: In the previous work~\cite{Tomonari:2024ybs , we counted the non-linear DoFs of NGR focusing on Type 2, Type 3, Type 5, and Type 8, applying the Dirac-Bergmann analysis~\cite{Dirac:1950pj,Dirac:1958sq,Bergmann:1949zz,BergmannBrunings1949,Bergmann1950,Anderson:1951ta .}}
elements.sent-2411-130: These types contain gravitational propagating modes~\cite{Bahamonde:2024zkb .}
elements.sent-2411-131: In particular, in the previous work~\cite{Tomonari:2024ybs , we also unveiled that a set of specific conditions on Lagrange multipliers may cause bifurcations, and we define a new class of constraint densities labeled as a semi-first class constraint density.}
elements.sent-2411-132: In Type 8, the semi-first class constraint densities exist and bifurcations occur.
